tweets.text:
The clock is ticking. The moment China blinks, it's all over.

China is not going to balance the world on its own. Those hefty US exports are about to fall off a cliff in July.

China would need to reduce its crude imports to 3 million b/d, -8 million b/d y-o-y, or -240 million bbls a month to balance the global crude market.

Good fucking luck, especially now that we are going into peak summer demand months.

Every physical oil trader on the planet is watching every transaction like a hawk. The moment China steps in, they are going to frontrun it like no tomorrow.

China tried to get cute, now it's going to get fucked. Communists, thinking they can outsmart the invisible hand, inevitably get destroyed. 

It is what it is.

Tom Lee could actually realistically trade his way out of the hole.

Step 1) Open massive OTM puts on ETH and short ETH perps on Hyperliquid 

Step 2) Buy massive OTM call options on HYPE (150/200 CSP)

Step 3) Unwind your ETH position publicly, and quickly with the announcement that you're converting all of it to HYPE

Step 4) Unwind both your ETH short exposure and your HYPE calls and bank 10B+

Step 5) Let HYPE spot appreciation take care of the rest

tweets.text:
a couple things from riding in waymos in miami this weekend:

def felt the waymo drove very safe.  didn't feel in danger once.

once we hit a certain % (prob like 20-25%) of self driving cars on the road, i think it will start to alleviate traffic congestion.  and then traffic will get significantly better the higher that number goes.

will prob significantly increase the number of electronics/components/memory used in cars, as everything will have sensors both inside and out.

the form factor of cars will definitely be changing.  i would've preferred being able to recline the seat more so that i could lay down or relax more.  also will change the nature of commuting.  i bet there will be workout cars, so people can work out on their commute to or from work.

i bet autonomous car acceptance happens faster than robot acceptance.  not as creepy.

you should prob try and buy a bunch of these when they rollout and put them into service.  i bet a utilization rate even as low as 50% would make these things insanely profitable.
tweets.full_text:
a couple things from riding in waymos in miami this weekend:

def felt the waymo drove very safe.  didn't feel in danger once.

once we hit a certain % (prob like 20-25%) of self driving cars on the road, i think it will start to alleviate traffic congestion.  and then traffic will get significantly better the higher that number goes.

will prob significantly increase the number of electronics/components/memory used in cars, as everything will have sensors both inside and out.

the form factor of cars will definitely be changing.  i would've preferred being able to recline the seat more so that i could lay down or relax more.  also will change the nature of commuting.  i bet there will be workout cars, so people can work out on their commute to or from work.

i bet autonomous car acceptance happens faster than robot acceptance.  not as creepy.

you should prob try and buy a bunch of these when they rollout and put them into service.  i bet a utilization rate even as low as 50% would make these things insanely profitable.
